zh.Humbie, East Lothian, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安比
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Humbie於1140年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾比。她於大約1160年與Malcolm de Keith在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安比結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1214年在她的家鄉享年74歲。

zh-hans.Humbie, East Lothian, Scotland: 英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安比
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Humbie于1140年出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾比。她于大约1160年与Malcolm de Keith在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安比结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1214年在她的家乡享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Humbie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): nickname for a meek or lowly person, from Middle English (h)umble, humel ‘humble, meek’, a borrowing of Old French umble, ultimately from Latin humilis ‘lowly’.

variants.name: Humbles
meanings: English: probably a post-medieval variant of Humble , with excrescent -s, especially in northeastern England, where Humble is chiefly found. However, early distribution of the name (predominantly in the South Midlands) is so distant from Humble that it may sometimes have a different origin, perhaps a nickname from Middle English (h)umbles, a variant of noumbles ‘stew or soup made from the innards of animals or fish’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Humbie was born in 1140, in Humbie, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. She married Malcolm de Keith about 1160, in Humbie, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1214, in her hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Humbie est née en 1140, à Humbie, Haddingtonshire, Écosse. Elle a épousé Malcolm de Keith environ 1160, à Humbie, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1214, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Humbie(은)는 1140년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 훔비에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1160년 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 훔비에서 Malcolm de Keith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1214년 그녀의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Humbie родилась в 1140 г. в местности хумби, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Malcolm de Keith около 1160 г. в местности хумби, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1214 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.
